name: AAdvantage® Globe™ Mastercard®
effective_cost: ~$100–150 net for a traveler who uses the Turo and inflight credits, before the year-two companion certificate

verdict.text: Only earns its $350 fee back if you actually fly American and use the Turo/inflight/splurge credits — a card built for a mid-frequency AA flyer, not a general-purpose travel card

earn_rates.category: Rides & rails — taxis, rideshare, public transit

earn_note: Launched by American Airlines and Citi in 2025 as a mid-tier card slotted between AAdvantage Platinum Select and AAdvantage Executive World Elite. Welcome offer is 60,000 miles after $4,000 spend in 3 months (a limited-time 90,000-mile / $5,000-in-4-months offer has circulated since May 2026 — confirm what's live before applying). A Flight Streak bonus adds 5,000 Loyalty Points per 4 qualifying AA flights, up to 15,000/year. No cap found on category earning.

redemption_options.method: American Airlines flights (no blackout dates on award redemptions)
AAdvantage eShopping, vacation packages, car rentals via aa.com

points.per_100k: ~$1,200–1,600 in AA flight redemptions, well less in non-flight redemptions
points.note: Standard airline miles, not a flexible bank-points currency — value depends entirely on American's award chart and availability. AAdvantage miles can also be earned by flying AA/oneworld and by everyday partners, so this card is best viewed as a top-up for people already in the AA ecosystem, not a standalone rewards engine.

transfer_partners.highlight: No transfer partners — this earns AAdvantage miles directly, a single-airline currency with no transfer flexibility in or out.

credits.name: Turo Credit
credits.subtitle: up to $30/trip, 8 trips/year
credits.description: Up to $30 in statement credits per eligible Turo trip, capped at $240/year, when the Turo account is linked and paid with this card.
tips: Only valuable if you already rent cars through Turo — otherwise this is the single biggest chunk of the card's advertised value that goes unused.
Requires linking your Citi card to your Turo account, not just paying with it at checkout.

credits.name: Inflight Purchase Credit
credits.subtitle: up to $100/year on AA flights
credits.description: Up to $100 in statement credits annually for inflight purchases (food, Wi-Fi, entertainment) on American Airlines flights charged to this card.
tips: Only accrues value if you actually fly American — a non-AA-heavy traveler gets $0 from this.

credits.name: Annual Splurge Credit
credits.subtitle: up to $100 across 2 of: 1stDibs, AAdvantage Hotels, Future Personal Training, Live Nation
credits.description: Up to $100 in statement credits per year, split across your choice of up to two of: 1stDibs, eligible AAdvantage Hotels bookings, Future Personal Training, or Live Nation, exclusions apply.
tips: A niche list — worth checking whether you'd naturally spend at any of these four before counting on this credit.

credits.name: Global Entry / TSA PreCheck Credit
credits.subtitle: up to $120 every 4 years
credits.description: Statement credit up to $120 every 4 years for Global Entry or TSA PreCheck application fees.
tips: Set-and-forget — apply once, get reimbursed, don't think about it again for 4 years.

insurance.detail: Enhanced Trip Cancellation/Interruption — named benefit; per-incident dollar caps not published on Citi's public benefits page
Named benefit ("Trip Delay") — trigger hours and reimbursement caps not published on Citi's public benefits page
Lost or Damaged Luggage coverage — named benefit; dollar caps not published on Citi's public benefits page
MasterRental® Coverage — secondary, standard Mastercard rental benefit; decline the counter's CDW and charge the full rental to this card
Named benefit — terms not itemized on Citi's public benefits page
"Purchase Assurance Plus" — named benefit; dollar caps not published on Citi's public benefits page

protection_note: Citi's public benefits pages for this card name six protections (Enhanced Trip Cancellation/Interruption, Trip Delay, Lost or Damaged Luggage, MasterRental Coverage, Extended Warranty, Purchase Assurance Plus) but don't publish the specific dollar caps, deductibles, or trigger thresholds anywhere in the consumer-facing pages — those live in the Guide to Benefits sent after account opening. Treat this as "a real, Mastercard-standard protection suite exists" rather than a promise of any particular dollar figure until you can read your own Guide to Benefits.
rental_note: MasterRental Coverage is secondary (pays after your personal auto policy) — standard for a Mastercard in this tier, not a standout. Decline the rental counter's own CDW/LDW and put the entire rental charge on this card to activate it.

status_perks.name: Admirals Club Globe Passes
status_perks.note: 4 single-visit, 24-hour Admirals Club passes per calendar year (~$300+ value) — not full Admirals Club membership, and they don't renew mid-year if used up.

status_perks.name: Preferred boarding
status_perks.note: Cardholder plus up to 8 companions on the same reservation.

status_perks.name: First checked bag free
status_perks.note: Domestic AA itineraries, cardholder plus up to 8 companions on the same reservation — can offset the annual fee by itself for a family that checks bags on 2+ AA trips/year.

services.name: Companion Certificate
services.detail: Starting in year two, upon renewal: one certificate for a companion ticket, costing $99 plus taxes/fees to redeem — a strong perk if you renew and travel with a companion, but you get nothing from it in year one.

services.name: AA Vacations discount
services.detail: 10% off eligible American Airlines Vacations packages booked at AAVacations.com/citiaadvantage.

services.name: No foreign transaction fees
services.detail: $0 on purchases made outside the US.

services.name: Citi Quick Lock & fraud monitoring
services.detail: Standard Citi account-security tools — lock the card from the app, 24-hour fraud alerts.

additional_cards.title: Authorized users

benefits.text: Earns miles on their spend (into your account)
Does not get their own Admirals Club Globe Passes — those are tied to the primary cardholder

additional_cards.note: Free to add and they earn into your mile balance, but the marquee lounge-pass and checked-bag/boarding perks are structured around the primary cardholder traveling with named companions on one reservation, not around a second full cardholder.

timeline.text: A limited-time welcome offer of 90,000 AAdvantage miles after $5,000 spend in 4 months has circulated, up from the standard 60,000 miles / $4,000 in 3 months — confirm which offer is live before applying, these promotions rotate.

American Airlines, Citi, and Mastercard launch the AAdvantage Globe Mastercard as a new mid-tier card between AAdvantage Platinum Select ($0/$99 fee) and AAdvantage Executive World Elite ($595 fee), built around Admirals Club day passes, a companion certificate, and lifestyle statement credits (Turo, inflight, splurge categories) rather than full lounge membership or elite-qualifying miles.
